book: Brihat Jataka English Translation- B Suryanarain Row 1919 LR

pages.text: Aries Lagna (Mesha): Under Brihat Jataka, the native born in Aries Lagna is highly courageous, enterprising, and possesses a high, active spirit. They have a hot temperament, scars on their body, and a natural fondness for travel. If the Lagna lord Mars is well-placed in quadrants, they attain administrative or military authority.

Taurus Lagna (Vrishabha): Under Brihat Jataka, the native born in Taurus Lagna is patient, practical, stubborn, and steady. They are fond of arts, music, and physical luxuries. They possess broad shoulders and a steady gait. Under the guidance of Venus, their life settles into material comfort in middle age.

Gemini Lagna (Mithuna): Under Brihat Jataka, the native born in Gemini Lagna is intellectual, dual-natured, an eloquent speaker, and possesses strong literary or scientific inclinations. Guided by Mercury, they excel in communications, writing, and calculations, but can experience emotional volatility.

Cancer Lagna (Karka): Under Brihat Jataka, the native born in Cancer Lagna is deeply emotional, intuitive, sensitive, and changeable in nature. They love water bodies, possess fertile imaginations, and are deeply attached to their domestic environment. They achieve success in public-facing careers.

Leo Lagna (Simha): Under Brihat Jataka, the native born in Leo Lagna displays a royal, dignified disposition. They are generous, broad-chested, possess strong leadership qualities, and natural executive or administrative capacity. Guided by the Sun, they command respect in society.

Virgo Lagna (Kanya): Under Brihat Jataka, the native born in Virgo Lagna is highly intelligent, analytical, discriminating, clean in habits, detail-oriented, and skilled in mathematics or literature. They possess attractive speech and excel in service-oriented or analytical professions.

Libra Lagna (Tula): Under Brihat Jataka, the native born in Libra Lagna is diplomatic, pleasant, artistic, balanced, and fair-minded. They possess a strong inclination for partnership, trade, public relations, and a love for fine arts and dynamic social gatherings.

Scorpio Lagna (Vrischika): Under Brihat Jataka, the native born in Scorpio Lagna is highly secretive, intense, courageous, occult-minded, and possesses deep physical and mental stamina. They have a magnetic personality, strong willpower, and can overcome major life crises.

Sagittarius Lagna (Dhanus): Under Brihat Jataka, the native born in Sagittarius Lagna is philosophical, active, optimistic, noble, and possesses a deep thirst for higher knowledge and long-distance travel. Guided by Jupiter, they excel as counselors, teachers, and religious leaders.

Capricorn Lagna (Makara): Under Brihat Jataka, the native born in Capricorn Lagna is hard-working, serious, structured, patient, and highly persistent. They encounter early-life struggles, but achieve high societal status, professional authority, and steady wealth through self-efforts in later years.

Aquarius Lagna (Kumbha): Under Brihat Jataka, the native born in Aquarius Lagna is humanitarian, scientific, intellectual, deeply analytical, and holds eccentric or reformist ideas. Guided by Saturn, they focus on societal advancement, networks of friends, and deep philosophical concepts.

Pisces Lagna (Meena): Under Brihat Jataka, the native born in Pisces Lagna is spiritual, intuitive, compassionate, artistic, and highly imaginative. They are drawn to mystical realms, fine arts, and display a gentle and accommodating character, succeeding in creative fields.

Sun in the 10th House: Under Brihat Jataka, the Sun occupying the 10th house is exceptionally strong, granting the native royal favors, high administrative status, leadership, robust health, and success in all career undertakings. The native achieves a position of authority in the state.

Moon in the 10th House: Under Brihat Jataka, the Moon placed in the 10th house indicates the native achieves high public reputation, popularity, wealth through trade or public interactions, and leads a highly honorable life. The native is widely respected for their gentle and compassionate leadership.

Jupiter in the 1st House (Lagna): Under Brihat Jataka, Jupiter in Lagna gives a highly dignified, wise, and respected character. The native possesses outstanding physical charm, good health, great learning, deep philosophical wisdom, and commands long life and substantial wealth.

Saturn in the 1st House (Lagna): Under Brihat Jataka, Saturn in Lagna represents early-life struggles, structural obstacles, physical fatigue, and a serious demeanor. However, if exalted or in its own sign, it grants strong self-discipline, longevity, profound wisdom, and eventual high executive power.

book: Sarvartha Chintamani with English Translation - B Suryanarayana Row 1899

pages.text: First House (Lagna Bhava): Under Sarvartha Chintamani, when the Lagna Lord is in its exaltation sign or positioned in a Kendra (1st, 4th, 7th, 10th) or a Trikona (5th, 9th), the native is blessed with robust health, a majestic physical build, great respect from rulers, a highly successful career, and freedom from disease.

Second House (Dhana Bhava): Under Sarvartha Chintamani, if the 2nd lord is situated in the 2nd house or associated with the benefic planet Jupiter, the native becomes highly wealthy, commands an eloquent speech, conserves liquid assets effectively, and enjoys great happiness within their family.

Tenth House (Karma Bhava): Under Sarvartha Chintamani, the conjunction of the 10th lord and the 9th lord sitting together in a quadrant (Kendra) or trine (Trikona) forms a supreme Dharma-Karmadhipati Raja Yoga, ensuring the native climbs to the absolute peak of career success, commands immense administrative power, and leaves a lasting positive legacy.

Sixth House (Shatru Bhava): Under Sarvartha Chintamani, if the 6th lord is exceptionally strong or placed in a Dusthana (6th, 8th, or 12th house), it cancels out sudden obstacles, grants absolute victory over competitors, litigations, and enemies, and ensures the native maintains good physical stamina.

book: The Astrological Self Instructor - B Suryanarain Rao 1893

pages.text: Planetary Aspects: Under The Astrological Self Instructor, the aspect (Drishti) of a natural benefic planet like Jupiter or Venus on any house cusp serves to protect that house and greatly enhances its auspicious material results. Conversely, the aspect of a natural malefic like Saturn or Mars brings delay, structural friction, and tests the native's patience.

Strength of Houses: Under The Astrological Self Instructor, any house cusp becomes exceptionally powerful and promises material success if it is occupied by or receives the friendly aspect of its own Lord, or if it is occupied/aspected by natural benefics like Jupiter, Venus, or waxing Moon.

book: Prasna Tantra by B.V.Raman - Raman Publication

pages.text: Ithasala Yoga (Applying Aspect): Under Prasna Tantra, if the Lagna Lord (representing the querent) and the Query Lord (representing the subject of the question) are in applying aspects (closer degrees) and both possess good mathematical strength, it forms a highly auspicious Ithasala Yoga. This indicates that the desired event will definitely happen successfully, smoothly, and rapidly.

Eshrapha Yoga (Separating Aspect): Under Prasna Tantra, if the Lagna Lord and the Query Lord are in separating aspects (degrees moving apart), it constitutes an Eshrapha Yoga. This signifies that the desired event will face severe delays, failure, or that the querent will be separated from the goal.

Nakta Yoga (Indirect Connection): Under Prasna Tantra, when there is no direct aspect between the Lagna Lord and the Query Lord, but a faster-moving planet (such as the Moon) aspects both of them sequentially, it acts as a cosmic bridge. This is called a Nakta Yoga and indicates that a third party will intervene to bring the query to a successful conclusion.
